summary: Sankalp HRMS backend full-suite pytest run: 26/27 passed; the single failure (test_health) was a transient 502 during backend hot-reload — manual curl 5/5 returned 200 immediately after, so the endpoint is healthy. All requested features verified end-to-end against the external REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.

minor.issue: One transient 502 during pytest run (uvicorn was hot-reloading). Retries returned 200 consistently.
Endpoint accepts arbitrary visit_type values inside the model_dump but only validates lead/project explicitly — fine since check happens before insert.
Allows non-admins implicitly NO (admin-only enforced). Note: GET /api/ledger has no role gate beyond auth — employees can list only own (already filtered).
No employee role test for forbidden — expenses require admin/manager (correctly enforced).

action_items: Optional: add an idempotent retry/health-warmup at the start of the test suite to absorb transient 502s during reload.
Optional: enforce explicit visit_type Literal in Pydantic schema rather than runtime check in handler.
Optional: cap working_days minimum > 0 in schema to avoid 0/None pitfalls (server.py already guards but Pydantic validator would be cleaner).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~1150 lines — consider splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/employees.py, routers/payroll.py, etc., for maintainability (>700 LOC threshold).
payslip_pdf imports reportlab inside the request handler — move to module-level imports to avoid per-request import overhead.
Ledger 'balance' formula treats deduction as owed-by-employee and allowance as paid extra — clearly documented in code; ensure UI mirrors this convention.
Attendance upsert by (employee_id, date) is correct; however payload.date is required while the model uses Date — no timezone issues observed.
auth_utils.py reads JWT_SECRET via os.environ[...] at import time — acceptable since .env is loaded by server.py before models import; works in this layout.
All endpoints correctly use AsyncSession + select() (no sync DB calls). MongoDB ObjectId leakage is not applicable (Postgres backend).

success_rate.backend: 100% effective (26/27 pytest; the 1 failure was a transient ingress 502 not reproducible on retry)

test_credentials: Admin: info.subhendu@gmail.com / Subhendu8958@ (seeded on startup). Test employees/managers are created with TEST_ prefix and cleaned up.
seed_data_creation: Suite creates a TEST_ employee and TEST_ manager, an attendance record (today), one lead visit, one project visit, one ledger advance (₹1000), one payroll for current month, one expense (TEST_travel ₹500). Cleanup runs in test_zzz_cleanup; payroll has no DELETE endpoint so one TEST payroll row remains per run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py for regression. BASE_URL = REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL + /api. Admin seeded automatically at startup. Storage bucket 'sankalp-files' is public and POST /api/upload returns a public URL (already verified). For frontend testing later, all data-testid coverage still needs verification; this run was backend-only per request.
rca_of_the_issue: test_health 502: pytest started immediately after a server hot-reload caused by file edits; uvicorn was momentarily not ready behind the ingress. Mitigation: add a 1–2s warm-up GET with retries before assertions, or simply re-run the suite. Confirmed not a backend defect (5 consecutive curls returned 200).
